text.title: llamaindex-ingestion-pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: Rules for LlamaIndex IngestionPipeline: composition, splitter choice, metadata extractors, vector-store persistence.

rule.statement: Always pass a docstore (SimpleDocumentStore or RedisDocumentStore) to IngestionPipeline. Without it, every run re-embeds the whole corpus.
rule.rationale: Docstore + content-hash dedup turns re-ingestion into a no-op when corpus unchanged.

rule.statement: Pick SentenceSplitter for structured/repetitive content (lists, FAQs, code); SemanticSplitterNodeParser for narrative prose.
rule.rationale: SemanticSplitter on structured docs over-splits into thousands of tiny nodes.

rule.statement: Run TitleExtractor + SummaryExtractor in the pipeline so retrieval has metadata handles. Without metadata you cannot filter or rerank by source.
rule.rationale: Metadata-aware retrieval lifts recall measurably on heterogeneous corpora.

rule.statement: Set num_workers ≥ 4 in IngestionPipeline.run for LLM-based extractors. Default sequential mode runs Nx slower.
rule.rationale: Metadata extractors are LLM calls; parallelism gives linear speedup until rate-limited.

rule.statement: Persist via StorageContext.from_defaults(persist_dir=...) AFTER pipeline.run; call storage_context.persist() before exit.
rule.rationale: In-memory stores die on restart; persistence is a single call you must not skip.

rule.statement: Hash document bodies for doc_id (not file path). Path-based IDs cause double-indexing on refactor.
rule.rationale: A repo reorganization re-embedded the entire corpus under new paths; cost spike to $40 for what should have been free.

rule.statement: For corpora over 100K nodes, use a hosted vector store (Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector). In-memory Chroma is fine for dev only.
rule.rationale: In-memory cannot survive horizontal scaling or pod restarts in production.

rule.statement: Batch ingestion runs with chunked reads (do not load whole corpus into RAM). Use IngestionPipeline.run(documents=batch, in_place=False) per chunk.
rule.rationale: All-in-RAM ingestion OOMs at low single-digit GB corpus sizes.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-advanced' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-high' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-low' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
